Description: Prevent backend 500s when Gemini confirmation styling fails during app-launch requests.
Notes: Added fallback-safe confirmation and direct-output styling.

Description: Allow open_app to open direct URLs and domains such as https://news.ycombinator.com, github.com, and localhost:3000.
Notes: Added direct URL detection and browser launch path.

Description: Cover URL launch handling, confirmation fallback behavior, and Windows launch safety with focused unit tests.
Notes: Focused pytest coverage added for restored launcher behavior.

Description: Skip the explanatory header row in applist.txt so it is not parsed as a real application entry.
Notes: Parser now ignores lines beginning with Format:.

Card Name: Verify app launcher behavior on desktop
Description: Manually verify configured app launch, browser fallback, and direct URL open behavior in the running desktop app.
Notes: Requires backend restart and local UI verification.

Description: Tighten user-facing copy for confirmation prompts and fallback messages so failures are clearer without sounding mechanical.
Notes: Keep wording short and consistent with Quacky voice.

Description: Create the main frontend shell for Quacky, including the initial desktop structure and user-facing application frame.
Notes: Frontend foundation built during the early UI push.

Description: Create the function that renders Quacky's icon for use across the desktop interface.
Notes: Custom icon drawing support added.

Description: Add the system tray experience so Quacky can stay accessible outside the main chat window.
Notes: Tray behavior wired into the desktop flow.

Card Name: Ship chat interface with speech input
Description: Build the main chat interface and support both text-based chat and speech-to-text interaction.
Notes: Unified chat UI supports typed and spoken input.

Description: Add the 3D model into its own window and connect it to the broader frontend experience.
Notes: Model window separated cleanly from the chat view.

Card Name: Build settings controls for 3D model and speech
Description: Create a settings menu that controls 3D model visibility and speech-to-speech behavior.
Notes: Settings menu expanded for major UI toggles.

Description: Implement a speech-to-text pipeline with 'Hey Quacky' wake-word detection for hands-free control.
Notes: Initial voice-first flow established.

Description: Support microphone selection with filtering for real input devices plus ambient noise calibration.
Notes: Improved device selection and input stability.

Description: Return to listening automatically after a voice command completes so interactions feel persistent.
Notes: Continuous listening loop added.

Description: Add a spoken quit command so the app can terminate cleanly without manual UI interaction.
Notes: Voice exit path added for hands-free usage.

Card Name: Integrate speech stack with AI backend
Description: Combine speech-to-text with the AI backend server and add chat session management for voice interactions.
Notes: Unified launcher and backend integration completed.

Card Name: Enable single-phrase wake-word plus command capture
Description: Capture the wake word and spoken command in one phrase to reduce missed follow-up commands.
Notes: Reduced dead space between activation and command parsing.

Description: Extend the listening window from 5 seconds to 15 seconds to improve command capture reliability.
Notes: Longer listening period reduced clipped commands.

Description: Allow speech recognition to remain active in a more persistent mode for ongoing voice sessions.
Notes: Reduced need for repeated wake-word reactivation.

Description: Refactor and simplify the speech-to-text module after the initial feature expansion.
Notes: Module cleanup followed the persistent activation work.
